verse_text: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
And the earth was without form, and void; and darkness was upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.
And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.
instruction: Let there be light.
notes: Creation command directed toward existence before mankind was created.

verse_text: And God saw the light, that it was good: and God divided the light from the darkness.
And God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And the evening and the morning were the first day.
And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.
instruction: Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters.
notes: Creation command directed toward existence before mankind was created.

verse_text: And God said, Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.
instruction: Let the firmament divide the waters from the waters.
notes: Creation command directed toward existence before mankind was created.

verse_text: And God made the firmament, and divided the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament: and it was so.
And God called the firmament Heaven. And the evening and the morning were the second day.
And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.
instruction: Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place.
notes: Creation command directed toward existence before mankind was created.

verse_text: And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.
instruction: Let the dry land appear.
notes: Creation command directed toward existence before mankind was created.

verse_text: And God called the dry land Earth; and the gathering together of the waters called he Seas: and God saw that it was good.
And God said, Let the earth bring forth grass, the herb yielding seed, and the fruit tree yielding fruit after his kind, whose seed is in itself, upon the earth: and it was so.
instruction: Let the earth bring forth grass, the herb yielding seed, and the fruit tree yielding fruit after his kind, whose seed is in itself, upon the earth.
notes: Creation command directed toward existence before mankind was created.

verse_text: And the earth brought forth grass, and herb yielding seed after his kind, and the tree yielding fruit, whose seed was in itself, after his kind: and God saw that it was good.
And the evening and the morning were the third day.
And God said, Let there be lights in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days, and years:
instruction: Let there be lights in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night.
notes: Creation command directed toward existence before mankind was created.

verse_text: And God said, Let there be lights in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days, and years:
instruction: Let the lights in the firmament of the heaven be for signs, and for seasons, and for days, and years.
notes: Creation command directed toward existence before mankind was created.

verse_text: And God said, Let there be lights in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days, and years:
instruction: Use the lights in the firmament of the heaven for distinguishing signs, seasons, days, and years.
notes: The command for humanity is implied because humans do not exist yet.

verse_text: And let them be for lights in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth: and it was so.
instruction: Let the lights in the firmament of the heaven be for lights upon the earth.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify the lights in the firmament of the heaven.

verse_text: And God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: he made the stars also.
instruction: Rule the day.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify the greater light.

verse_text: And God made two great lights; the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night: he made the stars also.
instruction: Rule the night.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify the lesser light.

verse_text: And God set them in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth,
instruction: Give light upon the earth.
command_receiver: lights in the firmament of the heaven
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify the lights in the firmament of the heaven.

verse_text: And to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness: and God saw that it was good.
instruction: Rule over the day and over the night.
command_receiver: lights in the firmament of the heaven
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify the lights in the firmament of the heaven.

verse_text: And to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness: and God saw that it was good.
instruction: Divide the light from the darkness.
command_receiver: lights in the firmament of the heaven
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify the lights in the firmament of the heaven.

verse_text: And the evening and the morning were the fourth day.
And God said, Let the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life, and fowl that may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.
instruction: Let the waters bring forth abundantly the moving creature that hath life, and fowl that may fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.
notes: Creation command directed toward the waters before mankind was created.

verse_text: And God created great whales, and every living creature that moveth, which the waters brought forth abundantly, after their kind, and every winged fowl after his kind: and God saw that it was good.
And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
instruction: Be fruitful.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify the great whales.

verse_text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
instruction: Multiply.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify the great whales.

verse_text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
instruction: Fill the waters in the seas.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify the great whales.

verse_text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
instruction: Be fruitful.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify every living creature that moveth.

verse_text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
instruction: Multiply.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify every living creature that moveth.

verse_text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
instruction: Fill the waters in the seas.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify every living creature that moveth.

verse_text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
instruction: Be fruitful.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify every winged fowl.

verse_text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
instruction: Multiply.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify every winged fowl.

verse_text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
instruction: Fill the waters in the seas.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify every winged fowl.

verse_text: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.
instruction: Multiply.
notes: While this order is maintained to this day, I chose not to personify every winged fowl.

verse_text: And the evening and the morning were the fifth day.
And God said, Let the earth bring forth the living creature after his kind, cattle, and creeping thing, and beast of the earth after his kind: and it was so.
instruction: Let the earth bring forth the living creature after his kind, cattle, and creeping thing, and beast of the earth after his kind.
notes: Creation command directed toward the earth before mankind was created.

verse_text: And God made the beast of the earth after his kind, and cattle after their kind, and every thing that creepeth upon the earth after his kind: and God saw that it was good.

And God said, Let us make man in our image, after our likeness: and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.
instruction: Let us make man in our image, after our likeness.
notes: Creation of humanity.

verse_text: And God said, Let us make man in our image, after our likeness: and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.
instruction: Let humanity have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth
notes: humanity's dominion of the world is in effect until God destroys the world.

verse_text: So God created man in his own image, in the image of God created he him; male and female created he them.

And God blessed them, and God said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it: and have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.
instruction: Be fruitful.

verse_text: And God blessed them, and God said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it: and have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.
instruction: Multiply.

verse_text: And God blessed them, and God said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it: and have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.
instruction: Replenish the earth.
notes: Original Hebrew meaning of this "replenish" refers to population expansion, not stewardship.

verse_text: And God blessed them, and God said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it: and have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.
instruction: Subdue the earth.

verse_text: And God blessed them, and God said unto them, Be fruitful, and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it: and have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.
instruction: Have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over every living thing that moveth upon the earth.

verse_text: And God said, Behold, I have given you every herb bearing seed, which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in the which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed; to you it shall be for meat.
instruction: Use for food every herb and fruit that bear seeds.
notes: This implied command was issued before any herb or fruit would be harmful; this was before the fall.

verse_text: And to every beast of the earth, and to every fowl of the air, and to every thing that creepeth upon the earth, wherein there is life, I have given every green herb for meat: and it was so.
instruction: Use for food every green herb.
notes: This implied command was issued before any green herb would be harmful; this was before the fall.

verse_text: And to every beast of the earth, and to every fowl of the air, and to every thing that creepeth upon the earth, wherein there is life, I have given every green herb for meat: and it was so.
instruction: Use for food every green herb.
notes: This implied command was issued before any green herb would be harmful; this was before the fall.

verse_text: And to every beast of the earth, and to every fowl of the air, and to every thing that creepeth upon the earth, wherein there is life, I have given every green herb for meat: and it was so.
instruction: Use for food every green herb.
command_receiver: every thing that creepeth upon the earth, wherein there is life
notes: This implied command was issued before any green herb would be harmful; this was before the fall.

verse_text: And God saw every thing that he had made, and, behold, it was very good. And the evening and the morning were the sixth day.
Thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all the host of them.
And on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made.
And God blessed the seventh day, and sanctified it: because that in it he had rested from all his work which God created and made.
instruction: Acknowledge the seventh day as sanctified by God.

verse_text: These are the generations of the heavens and of the earth when they were created, in the day that the Lord God made the earth and the heavens,

And every plant of the field before it was in the earth, and every herb of the field before it grew: for the Lord God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was not a man to till the ground.
But there went up a mist from the earth, and watered the whole face of the ground.
And the Lord God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
And the Lord God planted a garden eastward in Eden; and there he put the man whom he had formed.

And out of the ground made the Lord God to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.
And a river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from thence it was parted, and became into four heads.
The name of the first is Pison: that is it which compasseth the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;
And the gold of that land is good: there is bdellium and the onyx stone.
And the name of the second river is Gihon: the same is it that compasseth the whole land of Ethiopia.
And the name of the third river is Hiddekel: that is it which goeth toward the east of Assyria. And the fourth river is Euphrates.
And the Lord God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it.
instruction: Dress and keep the garden of Eden.

verse_text: And the Lord God commanded the man, saying, Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat:
instruction: Eat from every tree of the garden of Eden other than the tree of knowledge of good and evil.

verse_text: But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it: for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.
instruction: Do not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.

verse_text: And the Lord God said, It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him an help meet for him.
instruction: Do not be alone.
notes: Since it says "the man" this only applies to Adam.

verse_text: And out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought them unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creature, that was the name thereof.
instruction: Name every beast of the field and every fowl of the air.

verse_text: And out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought them unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creature, that was the name thereof.
instruction: Be named what Adam names you.

verse_text: And out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought them unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creature, that was the name thereof.
instruction: Be named what Adam names you.

verse_text: And out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field, and every fowl of the air; and brought them unto Adam to see what he would call them: and whatsoever Adam called every living creature, that was the name thereof.
instruction: Be named what Adam names you.

verse_text: And Adam gave names to all cattle, and to the fowl of the air, and to every beast of the field; but for Adam there was not found an help meet for him.
instruction: Be named what Adam names you.

verse_text: And Adam gave names to all cattle, and to the fowl of the air, and to every beast of the field; but for Adam there was not found an help meet for him.
instruction: Be named what Adam names you.

verse_text: And Adam gave names to all cattle, and to the fowl of the air, and to every beast of the field; but for Adam there was not found an help meet for him.
instruction: Be named what Adam names you.

verse_text: And the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam, and he slept: and he took one of his ribs, and closed up the flesh instead thereof;
And the rib, which the Lord God had taken from man, made he a woman, and brought her unto the man.
instruction: Be near Adam.

verse_text: And Adam said, This is now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh: she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.
instruction: Be called Woman.
notes: The command uses indirect language. Nevertheless, it is a direct command anyway, not implied.

verse_text: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.
instruction: Leave your father and mother.
notes: Paul would later say that to be unmarried is also a valid lifestyle in 1 Corinthians. So this verse is applicable today as a an established order of things, but implied and nonbinding as a command.

verse_text: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.
instruction: Cleave unto your wife.
notes: Paul would later say that to be unmarried is also a valid lifestyle in 1 Corinthians. So this verse is applicable today as a an established order of things, but implied and nonbinding as a command.

verse_text: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.
instruction: Be one flesh with your wife.
notes: Paul would later say that to be unmarried is also a valid lifestyle in 1 Corinthians. So this verse is applicable today as a an established order of things, but implied and nonbinding as a command.

verse_text: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife: and they shall be one flesh.
instruction: Be one flesh with your husband.
notes: Paul would later say that to be unmarried is also a valid lifestyle in 1 Corinthians. So this verse is applicable today as a an established order of things, but implied and nonbinding as a command.

verse_text: And they were both naked, the man and his wife, and were not ashamed.
Now the serpent was more subtil than any beast of the field which the Lord God had made. And he said unto the woman, Yea, hath God said, Ye shall not eat of every tree of the garden?
And the woman said unto the serpent, We may eat of the fruit of the trees of the garden:
But of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden, God hath said, Ye shall not eat of it, neither shall ye touch it, lest ye die.
instruction: Do not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
notes: Including Eve's recount

verse_text: And the serpent said unto the woman, Ye shall not surely die:
For God doth know that in the day ye eat thereof, then your eyes shall be opened, and ye shall be as gods, knowing good and evil.

And when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was pleasant to the eyes, and a tree to be desired to make one wise, she took of the fruit thereof, and did eat, and gave also unto her husband with her; and he did eat.
And the eyes of them both were opened, and they knew that they were naked; and they sewed fig leaves together, and made themselves aprons.
And they heard the voice of the Lord God walking in the garden in the cool of the day: and Adam and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the Lord God amongst the trees of the garden.
And the Lord God called unto Adam, and said unto him, Where art thou?
And he said, I heard thy voice in the garden, and I was afraid, because I was naked; and I hid myself.
And he said, Who told thee that thou wast naked? Hast thou eaten of the tree, whereof I commanded thee that thou shouldest not eat?
instruction: Do not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
notes: Repeat

verse_text: And the man said, The woman whom thou gavest to be with me, she gave me of the tree, and I did eat.
And the Lord God said unto the woman, What is this that thou hast done? And the woman said, The serpent beguiled me, and I did eat.

And the Lord God said unto the serpent, Because thou hast done this, thou art cursed above all cattle, and above every beast of the field; upon thy belly shalt thou go, and dust shalt thou eat all the days of thy life:
instruction: Be cursed above all cattle and above every beast of the field.
notes: Curse still in effect. The serpent is understood to be Satan.

verse_text: And the Lord God said unto the serpent, Because thou hast done this, thou art cursed above all cattle, and above every beast of the field; upon thy belly shalt thou go, and dust shalt thou eat all the days of thy life:
instruction: Go upon thy belly.
notes: Curse still in effect. The serpent is understood to be Satan.

verse_text: And the Lord God said unto the serpent, Because thou hast done this, thou art cursed above all cattle, and above every beast of the field; upon thy belly shalt thou go, and dust shalt thou eat all the days of thy life:
instruction: Eat dust all the days of thy life.
notes: Curse still in effect. The serpent is understood to be Satan.

verse_text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
instruction: Have enmity with the serpent, Satan.

verse_text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
instruction: Have enmity with Eve

verse_text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
instruction: Have enmity with serpents, the followers of Satan.

verse_text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
instruction: Have enmity with humanity.

verse_text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
instruction: Have enmity with serpents, the followers of Satan.
notes: Jesus is understood to be the seed of Eve in this verse.

verse_text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
instruction: Have enmity with Jesus.
notes: Jesus is understood to be the seed of Eve in this verse.

verse_text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
instruction: Bruise the head of the serpent, Satan.

verse_text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
instruction: Bruise the head of the serpent, Satan.
notes: Jesus is understood to be the seed of Eve in this verse.

verse_text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
instruction: Bruise the heel of humanity.

verse_text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
instruction: Bruise the heel of Jesus.
notes: Jesus is understood to be the seed of Eve in this verse. This is no longer applicable because it happened already when Jesus was harmed.

verse_text: Unto the woman he said, I will greatly multiply thy sorrow and thy conception; in sorrow thou shalt bring forth children; and thy desire shall be to thy husband, and he shall rule over thee.
instruction: Bring forth children in sorrow.

verse_text: Unto the woman he said, I will greatly multiply thy sorrow and thy conception; in sorrow thou shalt bring forth children; and thy desire shall be to thy husband, and he shall rule over thee.
instruction: Desire your husband.
notes: This may refer to a desire to dominate the husband.

verse_text: Unto the woman he said, I will greatly multiply thy sorrow and thy conception; in sorrow thou shalt bring forth children; and thy desire shall be to thy husband, and he shall rule over thee.
instruction: Rule over your wife.

verse_text: And unto Adam he said, Because thou hast hearkened unto the voice of thy wife, and hast eaten of the tree, of which I commanded thee, saying, Thou shalt not eat of it: cursed is the ground for thy sake; in sorrow shalt thou eat of it all the days of thy life;
instruction: Do not eat from the tree of knowledge of good and evil.
notes: Repeat

verse_text: And unto Adam he said, Because thou hast hearkened unto the voice of thy wife, and hast eaten of the tree, of which I commanded thee, saying, Thou shalt not eat of it: cursed is the ground for thy sake; in sorrow shalt thou eat of it all the days of thy life;
instruction: Be cursed for Adam's sake.

verse_text: And unto Adam he said, Because thou hast hearkened unto the voice of thy wife, and hast eaten of the tree, of which I commanded thee, saying, Thou shalt not eat of it: cursed is the ground for thy sake; in sorrow shalt thou eat of it all the days of thy life;
instruction: Eat of the cursed ground all the days of thy life.

verse_text: Thorns also and thistles shall it bring forth to thee; and thou shalt eat the herb of the field;
instruction: Bring forth thorns and thistles to Adam.

verse_text: Thorns also and thistles shall it bring forth to thee; and thou shalt eat the herb of the field;
instruction: Eat the herb of the field.

verse_text: In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return unto the ground; for out of it wast thou taken: for dust thou art, and unto dust shalt thou return.
instruction: Eat bread while working hard and sweating until you die and return to the ground.

verse_text: In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return unto the ground; for out of it wast thou taken: for dust thou art, and unto dust shalt thou return.
instruction: Eat bread while working hard and sweating until you die and return to the ground.

verse_text: In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return unto the ground; for out of it wast thou taken: for dust thou art, and unto dust shalt thou return.
instruction: Return to dust.

verse_text: In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return unto the ground; for out of it wast thou taken: for dust thou art, and unto dust shalt thou return.
instruction: Return to dust.

verse_text: And Adam called his wife's name Eve; because she was the mother of all living.
instruction: Be named Eve.
notes: I don't 100% know whether she still goes by the same name.

verse_text: Unto Adam also and to his wife did the Lord God make coats of skins, and clothed them.
instruction: Wear clothes.

verse_text: Unto Adam also and to his wife did the Lord God make coats of skins, and clothed them.
instruction: Wear clothes.

verse_text: Unto Adam also and to his wife did the Lord God make coats of skins, and clothed them.
instruction: Wear clothes.
notes: This is speculative on my part since Adam and Eve are stand-ins for humanity.

verse_text: And the Lord God said, Behold, the man is become as one of us, to know good and evil: and now, lest he put forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever:
instruction: Do not eat from the tree of life.
notes: verse goes with Genesis 3:23

verse_text: And the Lord God said, Behold, the man is become as one of us, to know good and evil: and now, lest he put forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever:
instruction: Do not seek to live forever outside of God's providence.
notes: verse goes with Genesis 3:23

verse_text: Therefore the Lord God sent him forth from the garden of Eden, to till the ground from whence he was taken.
instruction: Leave the garden of Eden.

verse_text: Therefore the Lord God sent him forth from the garden of Eden, to till the ground from whence he was taken.
instruction: Till the ground.

verse_text: So he drove out the man; and he placed at the east of the garden of Eden Cherubims, and a flaming sword which turned every way, to keep the way of the tree of life.
instruction: Leave the garden of Eden.

verse_text: So he drove out the man; and he placed at the east of the garden of Eden Cherubims, and a flaming sword which turned every way, to keep the way of the tree of life.
instruction: Keep the way of the tree of life.
notes: Unknown whether this command is still in effect or if the Cherubims were assigned a different task later.

verse_text: So he drove out the man; and he placed at the east of the garden of Eden Cherubims, and a flaming sword which turned every way, to keep the way of the tree of life.
instruction: Keep the way of the tree of life.
notes: Unknown whether this command is still in effect or if the flaming sword was assigned a different task later.

verse_text: And Adam knew Eve his wife; and she conceived, and bare Cain, and said, I have gotten a man from the Lord.
And she again bare his brother Abel. And Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground.
And in process of time it came to pass, that Cain brought of the fruit of the ground an offering unto the Lord.
And Abel, he also brought of the firstlings of his flock and of the fat thereof. And the Lord had respect unto Abel and to his offering:
instruction: Offer to God the firstlings of your flock and of the fat thereof.
notes: This is approximately where Adamic covenant related content stops. It suggests that God likes animal sacrifice but the command itself is not recorded. Also, this implied command no longer applies because of Jesus's sacrifice.

verse_text: But unto Cain and to his offering he had not respect. And Cain was very wroth, and his countenance fell.
instruction: Do not offer to God the fruit of the ground.

verse_text: And the Lord said unto Cain, Why art thou wroth? and why is thy countenance fallen?
If thou doest well, shalt thou not be accepted? and if thou doest not well, sin lieth at the door. And unto thee shall be his desire, and thou shalt rule over him.
instruction: Do well in order to be accepted by God.

verse_text: If thou doest well, shalt thou not be accepted? and if thou doest not well, sin lieth at the door. And unto thee shall be his desire, and thou shalt rule over him.
instruction: Do not let sin rule over you.

verse_text: And Cain talked with Abel his brother: and it came to pass, when they were in the field, that Cain rose up against Abel his brother, and slew him.
instruction: Do not murder.
notes: Part of story. Notably, the first human ever born was a murderer, signaling the fall.

verse_text: And the Lord said unto Cain, Where is Abel thy brother? And he said, I know not: Am I my brother's keeper?
instruction: Do not lie.
notes: Part of story.

verse_text: And he said, What hast thou done? the voice of thy brother's blood crieth unto me from the ground.
instruction: Do not murder.
notes: Part of story.

verse_text: And he said, What hast thou done? the voice of thy brother's blood crieth unto me from the ground.
instruction: Do not murder.
notes: Part of story.

verse_text: And now art thou cursed from the earth, which hath opened her mouth to receive thy brother's blood from thy hand;
instruction: Be cursed from the earth.

verse_text: And now art thou cursed from the earth, which hath opened her mouth to receive thy brother's blood from thy hand;
instruction: Do not murder.

verse_text: And now art thou cursed from the earth, which hath opened her mouth to receive thy brother's blood from thy hand;
instruction: Do not murder.

verse_text: When thou tillest the ground, it shall not henceforth yield unto thee her strength; a fugitive and a vagabond shalt thou be in the earth.
instruction: Struggle with tilling the ground.

verse_text: When thou tillest the ground, it shall not henceforth yield unto thee her strength; a fugitive and a vagabond shalt thou be in the earth.
instruction: Be a fugitive and a vagabond in the earth.

verse_text: And Cain said unto the Lord, My punishment is greater than I can bear.
instruction: Do not punish me.

verse_text: Behold, thou hast driven me out this day from the face of the earth; and from thy face shall I be hid; and I shall be a fugitive and a vagabond in the earth; and it shall come to pass, that every one that findeth me shall slay me.
And the Lord said unto him, Therefore whosoever slayeth Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold. And the Lord set a mark upon Cain, lest any finding him should kill him.
instruction: Do not slay Cain.

verse_text: And Cain went out from the presence of the Lord, and dwelt in the land of Nod, on the east of Eden.
And Cain knew his wife; and she conceived, and bare Enoch: and he builded a city, and called the name of the city, after the name of his son, Enoch.
And unto Enoch was born Irad: and Irad begat Mehujael: and Mehujael begat Methusael: and Methusael begat Lamech.
And Lamech took unto him two wives: the name of the one was Adah, and the name of the other Zillah.
And Adah bare Jabal: he was the father of such as dwell in tents, and of such as have cattle.
And his brother's name was Jubal: he was the father of all such as handle the harp and organ.
And Zillah, she also bare Tubalcain, an instructer of every artificer in brass and iron: and the sister of Tubalcain was Naamah.
And Lamech said unto his wives, Adah and Zillah, Hear my voice; ye wives of Lamech, hearken unto my speech: for I have slain a man to my wounding, and a young man to my hurt.
instruction: Listen to me.

verse_text: And Lamech said unto his wives, Adah and Zillah, Hear my voice; ye wives of Lamech, hearken unto my speech: for I have slain a man to my wounding, and a young man to my hurt.
instruction: Do not murder.

verse_text: If Cain shall be avenged sevenfold, truly Lamech seventy and sevenfold.
instruction: Do not kill me.
notes: Does he want Cain's mark? I am speculating the underlying meaning is "Do not kill me".

verse_text: And Adam knew his wife again; and she bare a son, and called his name Seth: For God, said she, hath appointed me another seed instead of Abel, whom Cain slew.
instruction: Name children with God's providence in mind.

verse_text: And to Seth, to him also there was born a son; and he called his name Enos: then began men to call upon the name of the Lord.
instruction: call upon the name of the Lord

verse_text: This is the book of the generations of Adam. In the day that God created man, in the likeness of God made he him;
instruction: Acknowledge that you and all of humanity are made in the likeness of God.
notes: Adam is understood to be a stand-in for humanity.

verse_text: Male and female created he them; and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created.
instruction: Be named "Adam" collectively.
notes: I do not know for certain if they still go by this name. The name Adam in Hebrew means "humanity" and is linked to the words "earth", "red", and "blood".

verse_text: Male and female created he them; and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created.
instruction: Be blessed.
notes: I don't think this is related to Adamic covenant. Blessing still in effect, probably, but since humanity’s condition becomes broken through sin and consequences, Adam and Eve suffered in life despite the blessing like all future humans would.

verse_text: And Adam lived an hundred and thirty years, and begat a son in his own likeness, and after his image; and called his name Seth:
instruction: Be named Seth.
notes: I don't know for certain if he still goes by this name.

verse_text: And the days of Adam after he had begotten Seth were eight hundred years: and he begat sons and daughters:
And all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years: and he died.
And Seth lived an hundred and five years, and begat Enos:
And Seth lived after he begat Enos eight hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters:
And all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years: and he died.
And Enos lived ninety years, and begat Cainan:
And Enos lived after he begat Cainan eight hundred and fifteen years, and begat sons and daughters:
And all the days of Enos were nine hundred and five years: and he died.
And Cainan lived seventy years and begat Mahalaleel:
And Cainan lived after he begat Mahalaleel eight hundred and forty years, and begat sons and daughters:
And all the days of Cainan were nine hundred and ten years: and he died.
And Mahalaleel lived sixty and five years, and begat Jared:
And Mahalaleel lived after he begat Jared eight hundred and thirty years, and begat sons and daughters:
And all the days of Mahalaleel were eight hundred ninety and five years: and he died.
And Jared lived an hundred sixty and two years, and he begat Enoch:
And Jared lived after he begat Enoch eight hundred years, and begat sons and daughters:
And all the days of Jared were nine hundred sixty and two years: and he died.
And Enoch lived sixty and five years, and begat Methuselah:
And Enoch walked with God after he begat Methuselah three hundred years, and begat sons and daughters:
instruction: Walk with God.
notes: The verse highlights Enoch in particular.

verse_text: And all the days of Enoch were three hundred sixty and five years:
And Enoch walked with God: and he was not; for God took him.
instruction: Walk with God.

verse_text: And Methuselah lived an hundred eighty and seven years, and begat Lamech.
And Methuselah lived after he begat Lamech seven hundred eighty and two years, and begat sons and daughters:
And all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred sixty and nine years: and he died.
And Lamech lived an hundred eighty and two years, and begat a son:
And he called his name Noah, saying, This same shall comfort us concerning our work and toil of our hands, because of the ground which the Lord hath cursed.
instruction: Name children with God's providence in mind.

verse_text: And he called his name Noah, saying, This same shall comfort us concerning our work and toil of our hands, because of the ground which the Lord hath cursed.
instruction: Be named Noah.

verse_text: And Lamech lived after he begat Noah five hundred ninety and five years, and begat sons and daughters:
And all the days of Lamech were seven hundred seventy and seven years: and he died.
And Noah was five hundred years old: and Noah begat Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
And it came to pass, when men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born unto them,
That the sons of God saw the daughters of men that they were fair; and they took them wives of all which they chose.
instruction: Be our wives.
notes: The term "sons of God" probably refers to heavenly beings who mated with human women, resulting in the birth of Nephilim. It is understood that God did not want this to happen. An alternative interpretation is the bloodlines of Seth and Cain intermarrying.

verse_text: And the Lord said, My spirit shall not always strive with man, for that he also is flesh: yet his days shall be an hundred and twenty years.
instruction: Do not expect God to tolerate sin forever.
notes: Although the flood punished those sinful humans and Nephilim, and Jesus's sacrifice has paid the blood debt for humanity, God still does not tolerate sin forever and intends to eventually end it.

verse_text: And the Lord said, My spirit shall not always strive with man, for that he also is flesh: yet his days shall be an hundred and twenty years.
instruction: Expect catastrophe in 120 years.
notes: This is understood to refer to the flood taking place 120 years later. It could also refer to a maximum lifespan of 120 years for a typical human, but exceptions exists.

verse_text: There were giants in the earth in those days; and also after that, when the sons of God came in unto the daughters of men, and they bare children to them, the same became mighty men which were of old, men of renown.
And God saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually.
instruction: Do not be wicked.

verse_text: And God saw that the wickedness of man was great in the earth, and that every imagination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually.
instruction: Do not imagine evil in your heart.

verse_text: And it repented the Lord that he had made man on the earth, and it grieved him at his heart.
And the Lord said, I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth; both man, and beast, and the creeping thing, and the fowls of the air; for it repenteth me that I have made them.
instruction: Be destroyed.

verse_text: And the Lord said, I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth; both man, and beast, and the creeping thing, and the fowls of the air; for it repenteth me that I have made them.
instruction: Be destroyed.
notes: I'm not sure why they had to be destroyed too. This excludes the ones Noah saved.

verse_text: And the Lord said, I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth; both man, and beast, and the creeping thing, and the fowls of the air; for it repenteth me that I have made them.
instruction: Be destroyed.
notes: I'm not sure why they had to be destroyed too. This excludes the ones Noah saved.

verse_text: And the Lord said, I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth; both man, and beast, and the creeping thing, and the fowls of the air; for it repenteth me that I have made them.
instruction: Be destroyed.
notes: I'm not sure why they had to be destroyed too. This excludes the ones Noah saved.

verse_text: But Noah found grace in the eyes of the Lord.
instruction: Be like Noah.

verse_text: These are the generations of Noah: Noah was a just man and perfect in his generations, and Noah walked with God.
instruction: Be just.

verse_text: These are the generations of Noah: Noah was a just man and perfect in his generations, and Noah walked with God.
instruction: Be perfect in your generations.
notes: This means to be blameless even when your peers (and people alive in the same general timeframe as you) are sinful and treat that as normal.

verse_text: These are the generations of Noah: Noah was a just man and perfect in his generations, and Noah walked with God.
instruction: Walk with God.

verse_text: These are the generations of Noah: Noah was a just man and perfect in his generations, and Noah walked with God.
instruction: Be like Noah.

verse_text: And Noah begat three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
The earth also was corrupt before God, and the earth was filled with violence.
instruction: Do not be corrupt before God.

verse_text: The earth also was corrupt before God, and the earth was filled with violence.
instruction: Do not fill the earth with violence.

verse_text: And God looked upon the earth, and, behold, it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted his way upon the earth.
instruction: Do not be corrupt.
notes: The sin of humans corrupts the earth itself.

verse_text: And God said unto Noah, The end of all flesh is come before me; for the earth is filled with violence through them; and, behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
instruction: Do not fill the earth with violence.

verse_text: And God said unto Noah, The end of all flesh is come before me; for the earth is filled with violence through them; and, behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
instruction: Be destroyed.

verse_text: And God said unto Noah, The end of all flesh is come before me; for the earth is filled with violence through them; and, behold, I will destroy them with the earth.
instruction: Be destroyed.

verse_text: Make thee an ark of gopher wood; rooms shalt thou make in the ark, and shalt pitch it within and without with pitch.
instruction: Make thee an ark of gopher wood.

verse_text: Make thee an ark of gopher wood; rooms shalt thou make in the ark, and shalt pitch it within and without with pitch.
instruction: Rooms shalt thou make in the ark.

verse_text: Make thee an ark of gopher wood; rooms shalt thou make in the ark, and shalt pitch it within and without with pitch.
instruction: Pitch the ark within and without with pitch.

verse_text: And this is the fashion which thou shalt make it of: The length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits, the breadth of it fifty cubits, and the height of it thirty cubits.
instruction: Fashion the ark with a length of three hundred cubits, a breadth of fifty cubits, and a height of thirty cubits.

verse_text: A window shalt thou make to the ark, and in a cubit shalt thou finish it above; and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof; with lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make it.
instruction: A window shalt thou make to the ark.

verse_text: A window shalt thou make to the ark, and in a cubit shalt thou finish it above; and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof; with lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make it.
instruction: In a cubit shalt thou finish the window above.

verse_text: A window shalt thou make to the ark, and in a cubit shalt thou finish it above; and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof; with lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make it.
instruction: The door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof.

verse_text: A window shalt thou make to the ark, and in a cubit shalt thou finish it above; and the door of the ark shalt thou set in the side thereof; with lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make it.
instruction: With lower, second, and third stories shalt thou make the ark.

verse_text: And, behold, I, even I, do bring a flood of waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh, wherein is the breath of life, from under heaven; and every thing that is in the earth shall die.
instruction: Be flooded.

verse_text: And, behold, I, even I, do bring a flood of waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh, wherein is the breath of life, from under heaven; and every thing that is in the earth shall die.
instruction: Be destroyed.

verse_text: And, behold, I, even I, do bring a flood of waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh, wherein is the breath of life, from under heaven; and every thing that is in the earth shall die.

verse_text: But with thee will I establish my covenant; and thou shalt come into the ark, thou, and thy sons, and thy wife, and thy sons' wives with thee.
instruction: Prepare to enter Noahic covenant.
notes: This is an expression of certainty. The command to join the covenant is later.

instruction: Come into the ark.

verse_text: And of every living thing of all flesh, two of every sort shalt thou bring into the ark, to keep them alive with thee; they shall be male and female.
instruction: Bring into the ark one male and one female of every living thing of all flesh.

verse_text: And of every living thing of all flesh, two of every sort shalt thou bring into the ark, to keep them alive with thee; they shall be male and female.
instruction: Keep the living things alive with you on the ark.
notes: It doesn't explicitly say "animals". I guess I'm speculating but it could be about creatures made of flesh that aren't animals.

verse_text: And of every living thing of all flesh, two of every sort shalt thou bring into the ark, to keep them alive with thee; they shall be male and female.
instruction: Cooperate with Noah to go onto the ark.
command_receiver: one male and one female of every living thing of all flesh
notes: The next verse says they will come unto Noah. I think it's implied that they will be tame as well.

verse_text: Of fowls after their kind, and of cattle after their kind, of every creeping thing of the earth after his kind, two of every sort shall come unto thee, to keep them alive.
instruction: Expect the following to come to you: one male and one female of every fowl after their kind.

verse_text: Of fowls after their kind, and of cattle after their kind, of every creeping thing of the earth after his kind, two of every sort shall come unto thee, to keep them alive.
